title: AGENTS.md
text: eulerr R package R orchestration layer Rust computational core (via extendr-api), built top eunoia Rust crate. User entry points: euler() venn() S3 generics (R/euler.R, R/venn.R) methods vectors, data frames, matrices, tables, lists. Input normalization: methods convert varied inputs named overlap vectors (R/parse_input.R + helpers R/utils.R); combination names joined & (e.g., "&B&C"). Fitting pipeline: fit_diagram() (R/fit_diagram.R) validates input, dispatches Rust backend via fit_euler_diagram() (declared R/extendr-wrappers.R), computes fit metrics (regionError, diagError, stress). Numerical engine (Rust): src/rust/src/lib.rs thin extendr shim converts R inputs eunoia::DiagramSpec, runs eunoia::Fitter, returns geometry back R. heavy geometry/optimization lives eunoia crate (src/rust/Cargo.toml depends eunoia). Build glue: configure invokes tools/config.R, reads DESCRIPTION SystemRequirements, validates installed rustc version, renders src/Makevars{.,.win.} → src/Makevars{,.win}. resulting Makevars runs cargo build --manifest-path=./rust/Cargo.toml produce libeulerr., linked eulerr.via src/entrypoint.c. setup_geometry() (R/setup_geometry.R) computes polygons/centers/bounds (uses polyclip polylabelr). setup_grobs() (R/setup_grobs.R) converts geometry grid grobs. Returns eulergram gTree drawn plot.eulergram()/print.eulergram(). package ships legend grob implementation (R/legend.R) rather using grid::legendGrob. Global plotting defaults: eulerr_options() .eulerr_env$options (R/eulerr_options.R, R/zzz.R) provide package-wide visual defaults consumed plotting code.

Instead, eulerr uses pole inaccessibility algorithm (Mapbox 2018) (originally Mapbox’s polylabel C++ library), implemented eunoia Rust crate powers eulerr’s geometry layer. label large fit inside region’s polygon, eulerr falls back one three exterior-placement strategies, delegated eunoia. strategy selected via labels$placement: "raycast" (default) labels placed along ray diagram centroid region’s pole inaccessibility, padded away diagram per-region margin. Closed-form, fast, deterministic. "force_directed" initial positions come raycast geometry, damped spring--repulsion solver relaxes labels foreign region polygons. Useful several labels otherwise collide drift across unrelated regions. "elbow" labels assigned left right column outside diagram, stacked vertically without overlap reached orthogonal three-segment (“elbow”) leader. Reminiscent d3-pie style callouts. every case, leader line drawn source region exterior label; tether leader styling controlled via labels$tether, labels$gap, labels$leader. raycast strategy places labels along rays diagram centroid, padded away diagram per-region margin. force-directed strategy relaxes labels foreign region polygons, useful several labels otherwise collide drift across unrelated regions. elbow strategy stacks exterior labels left/right columns reached orthogonal three-segment leader, style d3-pie callouts. Strategy-specific knobs live sublists. force-directed solver takes labels$force_directed = list(iterations = ...) elbow placement takes labels$elbow = list(margin = ..., min_gap = ...), min_gap sets minimum vertical centre--centre spacing stacked label boxes column: Elbow leaders extra vertical breathing room labels
code: set.seed(1) # A chain of four sets with long names and very small overlaps — the # overlap regions are too narrow to fit their quantity labels inside, # so all three strategies push the overlap labels to the exterior. fit <- euler(c(   Animalia = 8,   Plantae = 7,   Fungi = 6,   Bacteria = 5,   "Animalia&Plantae" = 0.05,   "Plantae&Fungi" = 0.05,   "Fungi&Bacteria" = 0.05,   "Animalia&Bacteria" = 0.05 )) plot(   fit,   quantities = TRUE,   labels = list(placement = "raycast"),   main = "raycast" ) plot(   fit,   quantities = TRUE,   labels = list(placement = "force_directed"),   main = "force-directed" ) plot(   fit,   quantities = TRUE,   labels = list(placement = "elbow"),   main = "elbow" ) plot(   fit,   quantities = TRUE,   labels = list(     placement = "elbow",     elbow = list(min_gap = 1)   ) )

title: Area-proportional Euler diagrams — euler
text: combinations set relationships named numeric vector, matrix, data.frame (see methods (class)) ... arguments passed methods input type input: disjoint identities ('disjoint') unions ('union'). transform function applied areas disjoint (exclusive) regions fitting. default, base::identity(), leaves areas untouched. monotone transform base::log1p() can keep small regions legible set sizes span several orders magnitude. transform applied exclusive regions additive atoms diagram fits ; consequence area whole set union longer equals transform() size, individual visible regions carry transformed scale. function must return non-negative, finite value region (complement, given). effect venn() diagrams, whose geometry fixed. shape geometric shape used diagram loss type loss minimize . default, "sum_squared", minimizes sum squared errors. available options mirror loss functions exposed eunoia Rust crate powers optimizer: "sum_squared" — normalized sum squared errors (default). "sum_absolute" — normalized sum absolute errors. "sum_absolute_region_error" — normalized sum absolute region errors. "sum_squared_region_error" — normalized sum squared region errors. "max_absolute" — normalized maximum absolute error. "max_squared" — normalized maximum squared error. "root_mean_squared" — normalized root-mean-squared error. "stress" — venneuler-style stress. "diag_error" — eulerAPE-style diagError. loss_aggregator deprecated; use loss directly instead. Pre-1.0 code combined loss ("square"/"abs"/"region") loss_aggregator ("sum"/"max") still works emits warning; combination mapped equivalent new loss value. complement optional single non-negative number giving area complement — , universe outside every named set. supplied, fitter jointly optimizes containing rectangle together diagram shapes area rectangle minus union (clipped) shapes matches complement. classical "everything set" region; see plot.euler() rendered. Defaults NULL (container; classical shape-fit). supported venn(). control list control parameters. extraopt: global-search fallback optimizer (CMA-ES) kick primary optimizer's diagError exceeds extraopt_threshold? default TRUE three-set ellipse fits FALSE otherwise. extraopt_threshold: threshold, terms diagError, CMA-ES fallback kicks . value 0 means kick error; value 1 means never kick . Default 0.001. tolerance: convergence tolerance passed underlying solver. Tighter values give accurate fits higher cost. Default 1e-8. max_sets: maximum number sets underlying engine accept. Defaults NULL, uses engine's built-default 32. Region masks stored bitset, values may raised 63 (absolute hard cap). Going higher rarely useful practice since fully-overlapping diagrams 2^n - 1 regions. weights numeric vector weights length number rows combinations. factor character matrix used base::() split data.frame matrix set combinations sep character use separate dummy-coded factors factor character vectors 'combinations'. factor_names whether include factor names constructing dummy codes

title: Area-proportional Euler diagrams — euler
text: list object class 'euler' following parameters. shapes data frame fitted shape parameters. One row per set type column (one "circle", "ellipse", "rectangle", "square"), center coordinates h k, shape-specific columns: , b, phi ellipses/circles; width height rectangles; side (plus mirrored width/height) squares. Columns apply chosen shape NA. ellipses shape = "circle" shape = "ellipse" fits, legacy 5-column data frame h, k, , b, phi. slot deprecated favour shapes populated rectangle/square fits. original.values set relationships input fitted.values set relationships solution residuals residuals regionError difference percentage points disjoint subset input respective area output diagError largest regionError stress normalized residual sums squares

title: Area-proportional Euler diagrams — euler
text: input matrix data frame argument specified, function returns list euler diagrams. function minimizes residual sums squares, $$   \sum_{=1}^n (A_i - \omega_i)^2, $$ default, \(\omega_i\) size ith disjoint subset, \(A_i\) corresponding area diagram, , unique contribution total area overlap. loss function can, however, controlled via loss argument. euler() also returns stress (venneuler), well diagError, regionError eulerAPE. stress statistic computed $$   \frac{\sum_{=1}^n (A_i - \beta\omega_i)^2}{\sum_{=1}^n A_i^2}, $$ $$   \beta = \sum_{=1}^n A_i\omega_i / \sum_{=1}^n \omega_i^2. $$ regionError computed $$   \left| \frac{A_i}{\sum_{=1}^n A_i} - \frac{\omega_i}{\sum_{=1}^n \omega_i}\right|. $$ diagError simply maximum regionError.

title: Plot Euler and Venn diagrams — plot.euler
text: x object class 'euler', generated euler() fills logical, vector, list graphical parameters fills diagram. Vectors assumed colors fills. See grid::grid.path(). Named fill vectors can matched fills$mode = "disjoint" (default) fills$mode = "union". patterns logical, vector, list graphical parameters fill patterns diagram. Vectors assumed pattern types (currently "stripes" NA), NA means pattern. Supported list items type, angle, col, lwd, alpha. Named pattern vectors can matched patterns$mode = "disjoint" (default) patterns$mode = "union". edges logical, vector, list graphical parameters edges diagram. Vectors assumed colors edges. See grid::grid.polyline(). legend logical scalar list. list, item side can used set location legend symbol_size can used scale legend symbols independently text size. See grid::grid.legend(). labels logical, vector, list. Vectors assumed text labels. See grid::grid.text(). addition grid::gpar() fields, following placement controls supported (delegated eunoia Rust crate): labels$placement ("raycast" (default), "force_directed", "elbow") selects strategy used label fit inside region. "raycast" "force_directed" produce straight leader lines (former places label along centroid→POI ray, latter relaxes labels polygon-aware force solver). "elbow" produces d3-pie style orthogonal leaders, stacking exterior labels left/right columns reached three-segment polyline. labels$margin (numeric) overrides per-region margin exterior label diagram (default half larger label's width height); labels$tether ("poi" (default) "boundary") chooses leader line attaches source region; labels$gap controls visible gap leader tip label box edge — bare numeric interpreted lines (font-relative), grid::unit() honored given, default NULL tracks eulerr_options()$padding gap matches spacing label quantity; labels$leader list (col, alpha, lwd, lty, lex) styling leader line drawn tether exterior label. Strategy-specific knobs live sublists: labels$force_directed = list(iterations = ...) sets iteration cap force-directed solver, labels$elbow = list(min_gap = ...) sets minimum vertical centre--centre spacing stacked label boxes elbow column. quantities logical, vector, list. Vectors assumed text quantities' labels, default original values input euler(). addition plain vectors, quantities$labels can also named vector keyed subset names (e.g., "", "B", "&B"), useful supplying custom text overlap regions. quantities$labels NULL, quantities$format can used control number formatting list item fun (function signif() round()) optional extra arguments passed function (example, list(fun = prettyNum, big.mark = ",")). quantities$total can used set external denominator percent/fraction quantities (instead plotted total). arguments apply grid::grid.text(), argument type may also used combination "counts", "percent", "fraction". first item printed first second printed thereafter inside brackets. default type = "counts". finer control rendered text, set quantities$template string {counts}, {percent}, /{fraction} placeholders, example "{counts}\n{percent}" put count percentage separate lines "n={counts} ({percent})" arbitrary layout. template set overrides type; set placeholders template determines values computed. annotations free-form per-region text rendered third stacked element quantity (label quantity drawn). Accepts named character vector keyed subset name (e.g. c(= "n = 12", "&B" = "n = 3")) shorthand list(labels = <vector>), list labels plus grid::gpar() fields (col, alpha, fontsize, cex, fontfamily, lineheight, font, rot). Regions absent labels annotated. composite tag bbox grows include annotation, exterior placement leader lines adapt automatically. Defaults slightly smaller text labels / quantities (cex = 0.8). strips list, ignored unless '' argument used euler(). addition graphical parameters, argument can include labels = list(top = ..., left = ...) custom strip labels. Unnamed labels interpreted display order. Named labels matched factor levels reordered display order. bg logical, character, list controlling background grob. Character values interpreted background fill color. main title plot form character, expression, list something can sensibly converted label via grDevices::.graphicsAnnot(). list length one can provided, case element used label. list longer length provided, item named 'label' must provided (used actual text). complement logical, character, list controlling container box complement region diagrams fit complement = euler(). TRUE (default) draws container dashed outline (lty = 2), fill, complement count inside complement region. FALSE suppresses container label entirely. character value treated fill color shorthand. list accepts fill, alpha, col, lty, lwd, lex (outline + label gpar), fontsize, cex, font, fontfamily, lineheight (label ), label (custom text — defaults complement count). Also accepts placement controls labels (placement, margin, tether, gap, leader, force_directed, elbow) complement count label. effect diagram fit without complement =. Defaults can set via eulerr_options(complement = ...). rotate numeric value giving angle degrees rotate entire diagram layout. Positive values rotate counter-clockwise. Defaults 0 (rotation). n number vertices edges fills adjust_labels logical. TRUE, adjustment made avoid overlaps --limits plotting labels, quantities, percentages. ... parameters update fills edges thereby shortcut set parameters grid::grid.text().

title: Plot Euler and Venn diagrams — plot.euler
code: fit <- euler(c("A" = 10, "B" = 5, "A&B" = 3))  # Customize colors, remove borders, bump alpha, color labels white plot(fit,      fills = list(fill = c("red", "steelblue4"), alpha = 0.5),      labels = list(col = "white", font = 4))   # Add quantities to the plot plot(fit, quantities = TRUE)   # Add free-form per-region annotations below the counts plot(   fit,   quantities = TRUE,   annotations = c(A = "mean = 35", "A&B" = "mean = 41") )   # Add a custom legend and retain quantities plot(fit, quantities = TRUE, legend = list(labels = c("foo", "bar")))   # Plot without fills and distinguish sets with border types instead plot(fit, fills = "transparent", lty = 1:2)   # Save plot parameters to plot using some other method diagram_description <- plot(fit)  # Plots using 'by' argument plot(euler(fruits[, 1:4], by = list(sex)), legend = TRUE)   # Per-panel styling with `by_group` plot(   venn(fruits[, 1:4], by = list(sex)),   quantities = list(     by_group = list(       male = list(col = "steelblue"),       female = list(col = "tomato")     )   ) )

title: eulerr 8.0
text: major milestone eulerr. 8.0.0 release introduces complete rewrite underlying C++ codebase, now relying instead Rust library Eunoia, library developed. highlights brings: added support squares rectangles ! Eunoia uses analytical gradients smooth loss functions, leads significantly improved performance. also swapped default optimization algorithm Levendberg–Marquardt (LM) method plus CMA-ES fallback. LM method invoked loss squared residuals, much faster previous nlm() method. CMA-ES fallback replaces previous use GenSA package, invoked whenever LM method reaches minimum ’s level error (configurable user). now several labeling algorithms ensure labels overlap parts diagram. result , able drop multiple dependencies: Rcpp, RcppArmadillo, GenSA, polylabelr, polyclip longer required, since rextendr Eunoia handle Compiled code algorithms natively. calculated intersections now sparse, means intersections actually appear either input output (diagram) calculated stored. breaking change means index intersections position intersection name guarantee get value. Instead, must check intersection exists first, implicitly value zero. allows eulerr handle much larger combinations, since never store 2^n - 1-sized vector anywhere.
